title: NZCI Flexi: Excavator Awareness
subtitle: Industry-Recognised Excavator Safety Training
description: Comprehensive excavator awareness and safe operation training aligned with New Zealand construction industry standards. Flexible online delivery for site supervisors, operators, and safety officers.

lessons.title: Types of Excavators
lessons.content:
**Standard Hydraulic Excavators:**
The most common excavator in NZ civil construction. Available in mini (1-6t), midi (6-15t), and large (15t+) configurations.

**Applications:**
- Trenching and drainage
- Foundation excavation
- Rock breaking (with hydraulic hammer)
- Loading trucks
- Demolition

**Long Reach Excavators:**
Extended boom/arm for deep excavation or working over obstacles. Common in waterway work.

**Amphibious Excavators:**
Float-mounted for waterway dredging and coastal work. Specialised training required.

lessons.title: Excavator Components
lessons.content:
**Main Components:**
- **Undercarriage**: Tracks, rollers, drive sprocket, idlers
- **Slew Ring**: 360° rotation bearing
- **Counterweight**: Balances boom weight
- **Boom**: Main lifting arm
- **Arm (Dipper)**: Secondary arm
- **Bucket**: Various teeth/edge configurations
- **Hydraulic System**: Pumps, valves, cylinders

**Controls:**
- Left joystick: Slew + arm in/out
- Right joystick: Boom up/down + bucket curl
- Travel pedals/levers: Track movement
- Auxiliary: Attachments

lessons.title: Daily Pre-Start Procedure
lessons.content:
**Walk-Around Inspection (Pre-Start):**

□ Check for ground oil/fluid leaks
□ Inspect track tension and condition
□ Check bucket teeth and cutting edge wear
□ Inspect boom, arm, bucket pins and bushings
□ Check hydraulic hoses for damage/leaks
□ Verify all guards are in place
□ Check ROPS/FOPS certification plate
□ Verify fire extinguisher present and in date

**Cabin Checks (Engine Off):**
□ Engine oil level
□ Coolant level
□ Hydraulic oil level
□ Fuel level
□ All warning lights functional
□ Seat belt operational
□ All controls return to neutral

lessons.title: COGS — Certificate of Competence
lessons.content:
**What is a COC/COGS?**
A Certificate of Competence (COC) issued under the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 is required to operate certain plant in NZ, including excavators over 4 tonnes.

**Requirements:**
- WorkSafe NZ approved training provider
- Practical assessment on the machine class
- Theory examination
- Medical fitness declaration
- Renewal every 5 years

**Machine Classes:**
- Class 1: Crawler excavator
- Class 2: Wheeled excavator
- Class 4: Backhoe loader

**IMPORTANT:** Operating without a COC is a serious HSWA offence with fines up to $50,000.

lessons.title: Exclusion Zones & Spotters
lessons.content:
**Exclusion Zones:**
No person shall be within the swing radius of an excavator while it is operating unless:
- A competent spotter is in place
- Physical barriers are erected
- The operator has direct line of sight

**Minimum Exclusion Zone:**
- Standard: Full swing radius + 1 metre
- Demolition: Minimum 1.5x machine height
- Near overhead lines: As per lines authority permit

**Spotter Requirements:**
- Designated, trained person
- High-vis vest mandatory
- Agreed signal system with operator
- Must never enter exclusion zone

**Underground Services:**
Call Before You Dig (0800 248 344) minimum 2 working days before excavation.

lessons.title: Trenching Safety
lessons.content:
**Cave-In Prevention:**
All trenches over 1.5m deep require engineered support or battered/benched sides.

**Safe Batter Angles by Soil Type:**
- Rock: Vertical (90°) may be acceptable
- Dense clay: 60-75°
- Sandy clay: 45-60°
- Sand/gravel: 30-45°
- Waterlogged: Shore or sheet pile required

**Working Near Trenches:**
- Spoil heap minimum 600mm from edge
- Vehicles minimum 1m from edge (engineered setback for deep trenches)
- Regular inspection by competent person
- Escape route every 25m of trench

**WaterCare/Council Requirements:**
Many NZ councils require a Confined Space Permit for any work in trenches over 1.5m. Check locally.

lessons.title: Emergency Scenarios
lessons.content:
**Machine Fire:**
1. Shut down engine immediately
2. Evacuate operator cab
3. Use fire extinguisher ONLY if fire is small and you are safe
4. Move all personnel to muster point
5. Call 111
6. Do NOT re-enter burning machine

**Tip-Over:**
1. Stay in cab — ROPS protects you
2. Brace with hands/feet — NOT against glass
3. Do not jump out
4. Once stopped — shut engine
5. Exit away from crush zone
6. Call for help

**Striking Underground Service:**
1. STOP immediately
2. Evacuate area (gas: 150m, power: 10m minimum)
3. Call utility owner and emergency services
4. Do not re-enter until cleared by utility
5. Report to WorkSafe NZ if injury or near-miss

lessons.title: Course Summary
lessons.content:
**You have completed NZCI Flexi: Excavator Awareness!**

✅ Excavator types and components
✅ Daily pre-start inspections
✅ COGS/COC requirements under HSWA 2015
✅ Exclusion zones and spotter protocols
✅ Trenching safety standards
✅ Emergency procedures

**Important Reminder:**
This course provides AWARENESS training. A Certificate of Competence (COC) from a WorkSafe-approved provider is required to OPERATE an excavator over 4 tonnes in New Zealand.

Contact Civil App for COC training referrals.
